query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Enshroud', 'To cover with (or as if with) a shroud', 'The author hoped the new policy would enshroud tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ensnare', 'To entrap, to catch in a snare or trap', 'Over time, the community learned to ensnare its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entail', 'To involve (something) as a necessary or inevitable part or consequence', 'Critics argued that the plan might entail the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entangle', 'To twist in such a manner as not to be easily separated', 'Critics argued that the plan might entangle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enthrall', 'To captivate or charm', 'The essay highlights the enthrall of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enthrone', 'To invest with sovereign or episcopal authority', 'Over time, the community learned to enthrone its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enthuse', 'To cause (someone) to feel enthusiasm or to be enthusiastic', 'The author hoped the new policy would enthuse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enthusiastic', 'With zealous fervor, excited, motivated', 'Scholars noted a striking enthusiastic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entice', 'To lure, to attract by arousing desire or hope', 'Over time, the community learned to entice its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entirety', 'Wholeness, fullness, the whole', 'Scholars noted a striking entirety between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entomology', 'The branch of zoology dealing with insects', 'The essay highlights the entomology of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entrail', 'The internal parts of the trunk of a body, the intestines', 'Her entrail became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entreat', 'To treat, or conduct toward, to deal with, to use', 'Critics argued that the plan might entreat the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entreaty', 'The act of entreating or beseeching, urgent prayer', 'Scholars noted a striking entreaty between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entrée', 'A dish served as the main course of a meal', 'The essay highlights the entrée of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entrench', 'To place in a position of strength, establish firmly', 'The essay highlights the entrench of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Entwine', 'To twist or twine around something (or one another)', 'The author hoped the new policy would entwine tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enumerate', 'To count out or list one by one', 'The author hoped the new policy would enumerate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enunciate', 'To make a definite or systematic statement of', 'Critics argued that the plan might enunciate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enunciation', 'The act of announcing, proclaiming, or making known', 'Her enunciation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Envenom', 'To make poisonous', 'The author hoped the new policy would envenom tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Envoy', 'A representative, a diplomatic agent', 'Her envoy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enzyme', 'A protein that catalyses a biological chemical reaction', 'The essay highlights the enzyme of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ephemeral', 'Lasting for a very short time', '“The beauty of a sunset is ephemeral.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epic', 'Heroic, majestic, impressively great', 'Scholars noted a striking epic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epicure', 'A person who cultivates a refined taste, connoisseur', 'Her epicure became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epicurean', 'Pursuing pleasure, esp. in reference to food or comfort', 'Critics argued that the plan might epicurean the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epicycle', 'Circle whose centre is on the circumference of a larger one', 'Her epicycle became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epicycloid', 'Plane curve made by tracing the path of a point of a circle', 'The essay highlights the epicycloid of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epidemic', 'Like or having to do with an epidemic, widespread', 'The author hoped the new policy would epidemic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epidermi', 'The outer, protective layer of the skin of vertebrates', 'Her epidermi became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epigram', 'Any witty, ingenious, or pointed saying tersely expressed', 'Her epigram became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epilogue', 'A concluding part added to a literary work, as a novel', 'The essay highlights the epilogue of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epiphany', 'A manifestation of a divine or superhuman being', 'The essay highlights the epiphany of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epistemology', 'The branch of philosophy dealing with the study of knowledge', 'Scholars noted a striking epistemology between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epistle', 'A letter, or a literary composition in the form of a letter', 'Her epistle became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epistolary', 'Of or relating to letters, or the writing of letters', 'Critics argued that the plan might epistolary the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epitaph', 'An inscription on a gravestone in memory of the deceased', 'The essay highlights the epitaph of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epithet', 'Any phrase applied to a person/thing to describe a quality', 'The essay highlights the epithet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epitome', 'A person or thing that is a perfect example', 'Her epitome became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epitomize', 'To serve as a typical example of, typify', 'Critics argued that the plan might epitomize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epizootic', 'Like or having to do with an epidemic among animals', 'Over time, the community learned to epizootic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epoch', 'A period of history, esp. considered remarkable/noteworthy', 'Her epoch became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Epode', 'A lyric poem in which a short verse follws a long one', 'Scholars noted a striking epode between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equable', 'Unvarying, calm and steady, constant and uniform', 'The passage presents a equable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equalize', 'To make equal', 'Over time, the community learned to equalize its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equanimity', 'Mental or emotional stability or composure, calmness', 'The essay highlights the equanimity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equestrian', 'Of horseback riding or horseback riders', 'Her equestrian became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equilibrium', 'The condition in which competing influences are balanced', 'Her equilibrium became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equine', 'Of or relating to a horse or horses', 'Critics argued that the plan might equine the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
